sentences.text: Hey there, William, how are you today?
I'm good, Phil, how are you?
I'm good, I'm good.
Busy, as you can imagine, short week.
What's so busy… what's so busy for you?
What you got going on?
Just when you take a few days out, you know, the correspondence catches up, and then a couple projects back up, and then, you know, it's just… it's a little bit of a train wreck.
Right, right.
How about you, man?
Are you looking forward to a long weekend?
Yeah, I'm… I'm out here in about 2 hours, and then, I'm off tomorrow, so… extra long weekend.
3 days was not enough, I had to do 4 days, so….
For you.
I just came back, and I'm ready for the next one.
Exactly, exactly.
Well, cool, cool.
No, I appreciate you, you throwing this back on the calendar.
Just had a couple things kind of wanted to talk through, kind of go through.
… it's been a quiet month for acquisitions.
We've had a disposition or two in there.
Trying to think if any of the dispos have been, really going through ApartmentIQ, or if it is a different, different vendors.
Let me see on some of the activity that we've had.
I think some of them are might… we have one third-party owner that It uses their own, one of the, come on, one of the competitors for it.
Let me see… yeah, so I think most of them have been… been with that, but, I sent an email this morning about Jefferson Place.
Did you see that one already?
I'm sorry, I haven't caught that yet.
Okay.
What's going on with Jefferson Place?
Is that just coming out, or coming in?
Coming in!
Both.
Both.
So, Jefferson Place, it's the same situation.
We've done this before, but, it was a disposition, a third-party owner that we managed for, and then we were able to retain management for the new owner as well, somebody that we already work with.
So just kind of wanted to… the only thing… comps are the same, users are the same.
Really, the only thing that should have to change is that peak code that's in there for it, too.
So, … I don't see any reason why that wouldn't all be ready to go for the first of the month for us on that.
Absolutely.
We'll get that done.
Cool.
And then looking ahead, I think our next acquisition, … that we have.
officially right now isn't until October.

We've got a couple things in the works, but next one is October for that, so, yeah, keeping us busy on the dispoad side these days, it seems like the bulk of it, so… That's where those… those kind of stand with us.
Excellent.
Cool So, ….
Another year and a half hanging out, huh?
I know, right?
Yeah.
So, … Yeah, that's… that's what those are, that's what we've got.

But just to kind of… the other topic that we want to talk through, I know that you know, those conversations that we had back a few months ago, with David and kind of the Corey and the bigger team and everything, I'm pretty sure we've signed our renewal, committed for that.
And then now we've got a couple new features and the new tools with being able to do the… what's it called?
What are the two things?
Research and explore, right?
Research and explore, yeah, that's.
Okay.
beef.
Right.
Yep.
So, we just kind of wanted to talk through, you know, I've gotta present something to…
up the ladder for how we're kind of rolling that out.
Yeah.
It's my understanding, we already have access to it now, correct?
That's right.
We just need to engage the research seats.
It's basically a toggle switch that our admin team manages.
Okay.
So… The research side, that seat-driven, explore side, everyone should have access to.
Huh.
What I like to do is make sure that I know who is going to be enabled for the research seats.
And then I need to… I want to walk through the features with them and the functionality with them so that they all feel really comfortable.
Okay.
So as far as rollout, that's what I would love to do.
And kind of the same thing on the Explore side.
Most typically, there's, you know, the Venn diagram of people who are using Explore and using research tends to be a circle anyway, because the tools go hand in hand.
So, I'd love to drive those conversations with folks once we kind of know who we want to be maybe our first key group using them, and we can kind of pull it out in a waterfall style.
Okay.
But at least having those first couple of users really comfortable with the tools and understanding how they work together.
Okay.
That'd be great, I'd love to drive that.
All I need from you is just understanding who that will be and scheduling, helping me to schedule those calls.
Gotcha, gotcha, okay.
Yeah, and that's, kind of the same thing that we had, and… because we'd love to get some… some kind of support for rolling that out, just kind of training and… and kind of helping them understand.
I know that, like, Megan had the question earlier this week on it, and I think that if she would have known what she was looking for, then it might have been even a little bit faster for it as well.
Right.
So we definitely want to keep that at the RM level and above.
For having that, that research feature.
With Explore, I know you said everyone has it.
But is that everyone based off of, like, their access level, or is that everyone That is a user, has that.
what's cool is I can't… I do believe I can turn that on for specific users.
Okay.
So if we want to restrict that access, we can.
We do.
Excellent.
Okay, yeah.
So I'll get that… that'll also have to have… come… happen in concert with the list of research users, so….
Okay.

… So… My question is, I guess, that right now, we only have two user levels, and I know that we talked about all the different ones and the other things, but we've only got admin and read-only, and I don't necessarily think that with rolling this out would be the time to change that, or to do anything different with them.
So… an admin is basically that RM, or the RTMs and above, that have that access level.
So… Would that be sufficient to, say, anybody who has admin access to give them the research and explore, and then the read-only would stay with just The rest of them?
So, when I look at the Bell account, I did see a mix of user permissions.
Let me just open this up here.
Did I… did I see a mix, or am I making that up?

there's… there's more than admin and read-only in there that I've… and I'm talking like I know what I'm talking about, but there's more than… more than admin and read-only in there, but I… I'm pretty sure we only… Have people assigned to admin and read-only.
And I think that….
you know, that was one of the things.
One time we had somebody that was assigned to manager instead of read-only, or vice versa to… instead of to admin.
Yeah.
So, I mean, that was that side of it, but… I mean, we can look at it….
couple of editors….
… Okay.
not very many, I think 23 total editors.
Can….
Can you share your screen, or can I log in?
Oh, yeah, of course.
Yeah, let's see if I can… Identify, like, who that type of… What that classifies at.
Editor, I think, so I'm just doing a control find here on editor.
23 total hits, leasing consultant here, assistant community manager.
What level is editor?
Like, what does that give them?
That allows them to edit the comps for their assigned markets.
Whereas a manager can edit the comps and invite users to those markets.
So, for instance, your RMs typically end up managers, because they have a set portfolio, and then as their teams change, they can go ahead and manage access from there.
Yeah.
So….
We're giving research access out.
in order to edit a research report, right, because when ApartmentIQ creates a research report, our algorithm creates a comp set.
Yeah.
And then, oftentimes, the typical workflow is that folks who know the area or who are learning more about the area.
come in and start making edits to this.
I don't know why this API key thing is an issue, but that's how a typical workflow works.
I would recommend anybody who does have a research seat at least be an editor.
So that they can come in and make changes to the reports that they're creating.
Can we just make… Anyone with a research seat.
An admin?
Yeah.
And anyone….
Yeah.
else needs… like, we need to take away those people that you just showed as editors.
Sure.
I mean, that's an ACM.
They need to be knocked down.
Understood.
Like….
We need to, … Yeah, like….
Never lost it.
I don't know.
She's… she left.
She's not even with the company anymore.
Okay.
So, she was in our marketing team.
Yeah, they need to be read-only.
I don't know how many that you got.
Community manager, I think I have 20… about 20 users tops.
Okay.
If you could edit that, and just… Change those to read-only.
Absolutely.
I wonder if those are just, like.
That is….
Because… somebody that is an RM has the access to give them To adjust their users, right?
Every admin can do that.
I wonder if that's the case, is that they created these users themselves?
Yeah.
And… because when I'm submitting them to you on onboarding, I'm only giving you read-only and admin.
Right, I imagine….
That's why we only have 20 or so of these people who are….
What?
Right?
Yeah.
Imagine that a couple of RMs are saying, okay, I'm kind of getting tired of these requests to make comp changes, I'd rather empower the teams to do that.
Yeah.
So….
I imagine's happening.
Regional manager is an asset.
Kelly?
Yep, she should be an admin, perfect, yep.
Alright.
Keep going, community manager.
But yeah, at this point, it would be easy.
I'll just say everybody who's an admin gets a research seat.
Everybody who's read-only does not get research or explore.
Is that fair to say?
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
Alright.
She only doesn't get anything, and admin gets those two things.
Okay.
… Perfect.
And then, if you wouldn't mind.
We don't have to do this together, but if you could take a look at this afterwards and see if there's anybody that is manager, and making sure that they're… Yeah.
Admin.
For that as well.
Appropriate to be.
To be admin, as long as it's, like, a….
Regional Transition Manager or above.
This is an interesting one.
Beacon Technologies Marketing Agency?
WMS Beacon?
I don't know what that is, that might have been something that….
They haven't lost.
added.
file.
Yeah, I wonder if that was just… Added by the marketing team.
Okay, so then….
See, this is the problem, though, is that then you're… We're running into this, of if we give everyone the right to do this, then…
Every now and then, we gotta come in and check it this way.
Absolutely right, and I was about to say that.
Is there a reason why we wouldn't want people to… the… because the next step down from an admin… an admin can come in and change anything that we've just changed.
They can change anything that I can edit as well.
It's kind of like the god level of access, we might call it.
Yeah.
Is there any reason why we wouldn't want a level down?
to exist.
Poorhead.
Like, they can't make these kinds of changes?
So you're saying, like, If they… if they were editor, could they still do that?
If they were an editor, the only thing they could change in ApartmentIQ are the comps to the surveys that they're associated with.
But I guess the editor would be a bad example.
The manager, let's say if we were making them managers.
You can still do that.
Let's so for the….
Yeah, if we make Steven a manager, then he can come in and add John as either a manager, an editor, or a read-only person to this portfolio that they have access to.
Got it.
So, editor might be the best role here, because at least those managers can make the changes to their market survey.
But they cannot add anybody in and create new levels of permissioning.
Okay.
Might be something for us to chew on here.
Who to… and you're saying who would get the editor access, you think, would be my… who makes the most sense for that?
RMs, I think.
Yeah.
Because I think what we want to do is maybe… RMs, in my experience, get the most requests.
inbound to them internally.
So, the easiest thing for an RM to do, I think, is manage their portfolio's set of comp changes.
And then we keep user access siloed, with a couple of key admins further up the chain, or exclusively on the ApartmentIQ side, right?

Like, that was… I feel like that was what y'all were trying to avoid, though, beforehand, is that… every time that there wasn't… there was a change for… this person needs a change to their property, and I certainly don't want them all coming to me, which is what they do, …
you know, I get that.
Periodically, I'll get that, you know.
Two or three times a month, where they'll ask me, and they'll say.
we were told you're in charge of apartment IQ.
Right.
And I'm like, I don't… no, that's Phil.
If we make them managers, we would just need communication, I think, on their end, that everybody who comes into ApartmentIQ on your team is a read-only user.
Yeah.
Right, because if they're an admin, they can make wholesale changes, and I think that's a little bit dangerous to give to everybody.
Yeah.
I just… Yeah.
I… I just….
Or we can manage this this way for now.
Also, totally fine, I just….
Yeah.
And it might not even be an issue, it might be that every so often we have to come in here and just change a few folks over.
Yep.
Sounds good.
we can.
Yeah, I mean, we've had it this way for several months.
Yeah.
And only 20….
catering.
23, so I mean, I think that's reasonable, too.
… I would say…
when you're checking to see anybody that's manager to make sure they're admin, like, any of those regionals or anything for that right now.
I know we've had a few owners that have been added lately, and those we discussed were….
read only.
We're firm on just being, read-only.
And they're not… and as it follows, they won't have research and they won't have explore.
I think that's the right call.
Yeah.
Okay.
All right.
Next steps is I'm cleaning this up for read-onlys and admins exclusively.
Admins will get the research report, and then admins will get my outreach to start maybe these webinars, where I walk them through the functionality.
Kind of a refresher for folks who are using the tool, new information for those who it's new for, but we'll start a bit of a communication process to them, like, hey.

this is the toolset you have, here are some reference materials that ApartmentIQ has, and here's the webinar series that will offer maybe two, three trainings through September, perhaps, or through October.
Yeah, I would say, you know, are you gonna run those communications by me before we reach out to.
Yeah, I'll run the copy by you, for sure.
Yeah, just to make sure, for that.
… Because we're talking about… You know, and we've got them as admins.

really, that's what we want to do, is we… I think it's great to do a refresher on it, but to also just kind of let them know about Research and Explore, having that new feature and that new functionality to it.
There's nothing different that they need to do, for this, it's just they have more capabilities in it now.
I know that we showed it to a couple of people, like Megan beforehand.
admins, I think you're gonna find… 75, 80, 85, somewhere in that count of people.
So… I don't know, you know.
if you've had, past experience with doing that kind of intro type thing, I would say.

I mean, do you… do you open it up to… All of those people for the multiple options, or would you recommend, like, let's break it down by half the VPs in one and half the VPs in another, or… or what do you think for something like that?

So, I was thinking in this case, we offer a specific set, maybe two offerings for that higher VP level, and two offerings for folks between RM and VP, because the questions and the concerns, I think, are going to be slightly different as you go up that ladder.
In the past, that's what's worked out really well.
Okay.
with this kind of a number of folks, we'd like the sweet spot we find for participation is about 20 to 30 people in a webinar at a time.
Once it gets larger than that.
People tend to go on mute, camera off, and check out.
So the smaller and more targeted, I think, is the best, in our experience, the best way forward.
But those are gonna be pretty much identical.
Because they're… the RMs and the VPs are gonna have the same access level.
Absolutely right.
I just think the questions could be different, and I wouldn't necessarily want to derail it.

You know, if a VP is asking, and this is just hypothetically, but if a VP's asking a very targeted question to a VP workflow, and it doesn't apply to half the group, I don't want to lose half the group in the call.
Yeah.
Okay.
Sounds good, sounds good.
Okay.
Yeah, I will take that back and kind of give them, you know, a synopsis of what we talked about today.
Yeah, I'll write something up for you so that you have a quick kind of reference on what I'd like to do, what we typically do, and then, of course, we can tailor that if there's any feedback.
We'd rather do it XYZ way.
We're very flexible.
Any other support you think you might need?
We have our training enablement team, they can whip together anything else specific.
Yeah.
that folks might need, but for now, I know we've got some very general one-pagers on research and explore.
We have reference guides for both tools, so I'm going to send you those as well, so that you see what I'm talking about.
We'll do a couple webinars, and then they'll Communications outreach, just to say, hey, this is new.
Yeah, okay.
But that's my plan.
Okay, yep, if you don't mind, just run all those by… by to me.
That's exactly what I needed, so thank you for that, Bill.
Sure.

and then just kind of send them over, and I would think, like, we're probably… you know, you want to put it on the calendar enough time in advance to let people know about it and everything, but I would say, you know, back half of September or beginning of October is likely what we're looking at at this point.
Terrific.
For hosting some of those, if you agree with that kind of time frame.
Absolutely.
Okay.
Absolutely.
When are you gonna be making your… I mean, it sounds like you're signing off in an hour and a half or so, so is this your meeting with your team next week to discuss this?
We are actually meeting in person, next Wednesday, to kind of go up a little bit further for that, but I need to send them a recap before the end of the day, of just kind of what we've talked about.

Sonya was gonna join this, this call as well, but, she's under the weather this afternoon, so, I was just gonna give them a quick update on that and everything, and then, Kind of anything that you have to send over, we can kind of probably talk about a little bit more in person next week.
Okay, fair enough.
I'll be sure to send you the high points of my plan, and then I'll send you more details, maybe later, as I get to it tomorrow, perhaps, with the details of everything.
You can talk in person.
Last question I had for you before you go.
We also have a data sync aspect, where we're now giving you guys access to the entirety of our unit-level data for your portfolio.
Do you know who the best contact is there?
I know in the past, we've shown it to TJ, we've shown it to some other folks on the asset side.
Just wondering if you have any insight on who best to reach out to there, or if I should just maybe reach back out to TJ.
Probably TJ, I would say for that, yeah, that's… outside of our use, and I think that would… would that be outside of the use from operations?
I mean, that's where I come in.
Would ops use it, or is that not something that they typically use?
Ops could certainly… I'm not sure… no, more often, it's asset manager folks, it's data-driven folks.
We'll say no.
We're gonna say no.
Yep, so….
Alright.
for that.
Cool.
Oh, no, it's been helpful.
I appreciate it, Phil, and … Yeah, I'll keep an eye out for some of that stuff you send over, okay?
Alright, William, thanks so much, have a great, enjoy the weekend, alright?
I appreciate it, you too.
Take care.

conversation.title: Bell Partners <> Apt IQ Reporting Check In
